source.text_summary: Creates a 75-day limitations period for Title 18 crimes, with 150 days for listed chapters, and bars prosecution after expiration.
source.exact_change: Add a new Title 18 statute-of-limitations provision: 75 days for Title 18 offenses except listed chapters 29, 37, 47, 111A, 113B, 115, and 118; 150 days for those listed chapters; accrual at the time of the offense.
source.evidence: "Any Crime under Title 18 shall have a statute of limitationsof limitations of 75 days"; "All criminal prosecutions tried under clauses (1) - (7)"; "The Statute of Limitations for an offense shall commenceat the time the offense takes place."

source.text_summary: EXPRESS AMENDMENT of 3 U.S.C. §301 (General authorization to delegate functions) — amends it to read a new expanded delegation text (adds authority to establish departments/agencies, suspend/transfer statutory authorities and personnel, transfer Capitol Police authority/personnel with congressional consent, require training/certification).
source.exact_change: Amend 3 U.S.C. §301 to read the quoted expanded text: retains existing delegation language and appends new clauses authorizing establishment of executive departments/agencies from existing authorities; suspension/transfer of statutory authorities and personnel; transfer/suspension of U.S. Capitol Police statutory authority and personnel with written consent of both principal congressional officers; and mandatory training/certification requirements.
source.evidence: “3 U.S. Code §301 - General authorization to delegate functions; publication of delegations is amended to read: The President of the United States is authorized to designate and empower...”
source.notes: THIS IS A REAL TEXTUAL AMENDMENT to a numbered USC section — must be an actual amend-existing-text, not a note. On PL 9-53's own repeal, this amendment's disposition must be resolved by the integrator.

source.title: Public Law 12-81 | The Integrity Act
source.text_summary: Presidential consultation and approval rule before withdrawal from treaties or organizations.

source.title: Public Law 34-240 | Literally Fixing Taxes
source.text_summary: Short title, severability, and immediate-effective-date language.
source.evidence: "Literally Fixing Taxes"; "This bill shall take effect immediately"
source.notes: Introductory matter only.

consolidated.title: Public Law 34-240 | Literally Fixing Taxes

source.title: Public Law 34-240 | Literally Fixing Taxes
source.text_summary: Amend the flat tax rate in the Literally Introducing Taxes Act to 28 percent and repeal inconsistent language.
source.exact_change: Replace the 20 percent flat tax language with a 28 percent flat tax and repeal inconsistent provisions.
source.evidence: "There is hereby imposed on the taxable income of every individual a flat tax of 28%."; "All language in H.R. 9 or any other Act establishing a tax rate inconsistent with this amendment is hereby repealed"
source.notes: The law is marked repealed/superseded and is retained only as history.

consolidated.title: Public Law 34-240 | Literally Fixing Taxes
consolidated.exact_change: Replace the 20 percent flat tax language with a 28 percent flat tax and repeal inconsistent provisions.

source.title: Public Law 34-243 | Judicial Integrity & Accountability Act
source.text_summary: Short title and purpose to transfer judicial misconduct complaint authority to the Supreme Court.
source.evidence: "Judicial Integrity and Accountability Act of 2025"; "To transfer all judicial misconduct complaint authority to the Supreme Court"
source.notes: Introductory material only.

consolidated.title: Public Law 34-243 | Judicial Integrity & Accountability Act

source.title: Public Law 34-243 | Judicial Integrity & Accountability Act
source.text_summary: Repeal 28 U.S.C. 353, 359, and 361, then replace chapter 16 with new complaint, review, subpoena, disclosure, and effect-of-conviction provisions.
source.exact_change: Replace chapter 16 with sections 351-364 as enacted.
source.evidence: "28 U.S. Code Chapter 16 Part I section 353, 359, and 361 shall be repealed"; "CHAPTER 16 -- COMPLAINTS AGAINST JUDGES AND JUDICIAL DISCIPLINE BY THE SUPREME COURT"
source.notes: The live XML reflects the replacement chapter.

consolidated.title: Public Law 34-243 | Judicial Integrity & Accountability Act
consolidated.exact_change: Replace chapter 16 with sections 351-364 as enacted.

source.title: Public Law 34-243 | Judicial Integrity & Accountability Act
source.text_summary: New chapter 16 provisions governing complaints, review by the Supreme Court, impeachment referral, subpoena power, review limitations, rules, disclosure, and felony-conviction consequences.
source.exact_change: Install the new chapter 16 sections 351, 352, 354-360, 362-364 with the enacted text.
source.evidence: "Complaints; judge defined"; "Review of the complaint by the Supreme Court of the United States"; "Referral for Impeachment Consideration"; "Effect of felony conviction"
source.notes: The replacement chapter is already integrated in the branch.

consolidated.title: Public Law 34-243 | Judicial Integrity & Accountability Act
consolidated.exact_change: Install the new chapter 16 sections 351, 352, 354-360, 362-364 with the enacted text.
